title: Tessera
description: A Wave Function Collapse studio that now runs in TWO and THREE dimensions. NEWEST — the SOLVER LAB makes WFC's normally-invisible *search* legible and steerable: PLUGGABLE OBSERVATION HEURISTICS (Shannon entropy, minimum-remaining-values, scanline, random) and TILE-SELECTION POLICIES (weighted, uniform, greedy) you can swap live, a CONTRADICTION HEATMAP that tints the board by where the solver actually struggles, live search instrumentation (propagation eliminations + peak search depth), and a BENCHMARK that races all four heuristics on the same instance over shared seeds and tabulates success rate, mean backtracks, depth and steps — with the Proof Lab proving each heuristic still yields valid, deterministic output and that the heatmap sums exactly to the contradiction count (the refactor is byte-for-byte identical to the old engine on the default path, verified across 7 tilesets). Plus a whole second engine running WFC in TRUE 3D, from scratch (no Three.js, no WebGL): a 6-neighbour voxel lattice with a real cube-group socket algebra (symmetric/flipped horizontal seams + rotation-indexed vertical seams, so rotating a tile about Y stays sound), three hand-authored volumetric tilesets — Terraces (a stacked landscape), Castle (free-standing crenellated stone towers), and Pipes3D (a conduit network whose round cross-sections join seamlessly across all six faces) — and a from-scratch SOFTWARE VOXEL RENDERER: an orthographic orbit camera (drag to spin/tilt, wheel to zoom), surface extraction with interior + back-face culling, painter's-algorithm depth sorting and Lambert shading. The 3D solver mirrors the 2D core's guarantees on six neighbours (support-counter propagation, min-entropy observation, arc-consistency purge, snapshot backtracking), and an in-app 3D PROOF LAB proves them live: socket symmetry, rotation-group closure, a tensor that matches the socket rule exactly, determinism, and the headline — every finished 3D solve is 6-neighbour adjacency-valid, re-checked the long way. A top-level 2D⇄3D switch picks the engine; the 2D studio is untouched. The 2D side: a from-scratch solver with the tiled AND canonical overlapping model, hand-authored tilesets plus patterns learnt live from a bitmap you draw, the Lens cell inspector, hand-painted constraints, live weights, PNG/WebM/JSON export, and a GLOBAL CONNECTIVITY CONSTRAINT (one-network or route-between-pins) with its own graph engine and Proof Lab. Everything written from scratch, certified the house way.
